data.title: IRI launches CoSort® 11 with Operational Governance, Cloud Integration, and more, enhancing data management with AI.
data.description: The technology company Innovative Routines International (IRI) has recently released CoSort® Version 11, which includes a novel Operational Governance System (OGS). This system offers granular control over data access and audit logging. The update also features AI-driven JCL-to-SortCL modernization, expanding enterprise data management capabilities.

data.title: OnviSource launches ChatOrchestra™, an AI for contact centers that shifts from software to workforce, enabling outcome definition through natural language.
data.description: OnviSource has introduced the ChatOrchestra™, a Conversational Agentic AI platform that revolutionizes AI technology by transforming it into a managed intelligent workforce for contact centers. This innovative tool allows companies to establish objectives using natural language and provides a dynamic mechanism for achieving them. With ChatOrchestra, organizations can streamline their operations and improve customer service, leading to increased efficiency and overall performance.

data.title: Osceola County, Florida introduces ASAP service reducing alarm call handling time by up to 8 minutes per incident.
data.description: In a recent development, Osceola County, Florida has implemented the Automated Secure Alarm Protocol (ASAP), streamlining alarm notifications. This advanced technology aims to alleviate pressure on 911 dispatchers while enhancing the speed and precision of emergency responses. The integration took place on January 20, 2026, marking a significant step toward improving the county's emergency communication system.

data.title: Financial firms are hiring consultants for project-based work, with Landing Point's consulting practice growing by 98% in 2025.
data.description: Asset managers are opting to enlist experienced consultants for fund launches, tech implementations, and regulatory projects instead of overburdening their permanent teams. This shift has led to a 98% growth in Landing Point's consulting practice in 2025. The firm foresees this trend gaining momentum in the future.

data.title: Bhutan plans to implement a Sei validator in Q1 to collaborate on tokenization. It operates a Bitcoin mining operation and a self-sovereign ID system on Ethereum.
data.description: Bhutan continues to expand its blockchain ventures, with a Bitcoin mining operation already in operation and a self-sovereign ID system driven by Ethereum recently introduced. The country is actively exploring the potential of blockchain technology and has embraced these initiatives as part of its digital strategy. This demonstrates Bhutan's commitment to leveraging innovative technologies for its development.

data.title: Grayscale plans to convert NEAR Protocol Trust into an ETF on NYSE Arca amid decreasing fund performance.
data.description: Grayscale recently converted its NEAR Trust into an ETF as part of its ongoing trend of transitioning trusts into exchange-traded funds (ETFs). This move comes as the fund's performance has been on the decline.

data.title: Crypto advisor Patrick Witt, appointed by Trump, urges compromises for passage of crypto bill in Senate amid GOP push for policy wins before midterms.
data.description: Patrick Witt, an advisor to former President Trump, has emphasized the necessity for compromises in the market structure bill to ensure its advancement in the Senate. Republicans are eager to secure policy victories before the upcoming midterms. This strategic move aims to garner support and push the bill forward. The bill's success hinges on concessions made to meet the Senate's requirements. By focusing on bipartisan agreements, Republicans aim to secure a significant policy win in the current political landscape. This alignment with the Senate dynamics is crucial for the bill's progress and potential impact, especially given the impending midterm elections.

data.title: Finance Secretary Paul Chan of Hong Kong advocated for equal regulations on cryptocurrencies at WEF, emphasizing the need for balance between innovation and control.
data.description: Discussing at an exclusive workshop in Davos, Finance Secretary Paul Chan emphasized the importance of implementing regulatory measures for digital assets alongside fostering innovation within the industry.

data.title: Rising number of new Bitcoin whales heighten risk of BTC dip towards $85,000 amid volatile market conditions.
data.description: Market analysts are suggesting that the volatility in the price of Bitcoin may indicate a growing possibility of distribution, particularly as new holders of large amounts of Bitcoin, known as whales, are influencing short-term market movements. There is speculation of a potential drop in Bitcoin's price to $85,000 in the near future.

data.title: Positive shift in ETH derivatives metric as net taker volume turns positive for first time in years. Will bulls boost Ether price?
data.description: After a drop at the weekly open, ETH experienced a shift as its net taker volume indicator showed positivity for the first time in several years. This change raises the question of whether bulls will seize the opportunity to drive the price of Ether up.

Markets briefly reacted to sell America headlines, then moved on. Stocks, bonds and the dollar saw mild pressure, but nothing lasting. The concern is that investors are learning to ignore Trump related risks altogether.

data.title: TomTom Traffic Index finds U.S. among least congested countries. 15th edition released on Jan. 21, 2026.
data.description: In Amsterdam, Netherlands, on January 21, 2026, TomTom (TOM2) unveiled the 15th edition of the TomTom Traffic Index. This report, a comprehensive examination of global traffic patterns and commuting habits, marks another milestone for the company.

data.title: Deutsche Telekom Security partners with Akamai to enhance cybersecurity with microsegmentation and API security.
data.description: Akamai and Deutsche Telekom have joined forces to enhance their cybersecurity services by incorporating microsegmentation and API security solutions. This collaboration aims to provide advanced protection against cyber threats and ensure the security of organizations' sensitive data. The integration of microsegmentation and API security technologies will strengthen their defense mechanisms, enabling them to better safeguard against increasingly sophisticated attacks. This partnership signifies a commitment to delivering robust cybersecurity solutions in the face of rising digital threats. The incorporation of these technologies will bolster their security offerings and enhance their ability to fend off cyber risks effectively. This collaboration highlights the importance of innovative cybersecurity solutions in an ever-evolving threat landscape. Organizations can now benefit from this enhanced cybersecurity suite to mitigate risks and protect their digital assets effectively.
Russia faces a sizable early-year budget deficit as oil and gas revenue declines weigh on government finances – IFAX.
KOSPI climbs 0.49% to reach a record closing high, driven by strong investor sentiment.

data.title: Wall Street analysts predict Halliburton will report Q4 earnings of 55 cents per share, down from 70 cents last year, with revenue estimated at $5.41 billion compared to $5.61 billion a year ago.
data.description: Halliburton Company (NYSE: HAL) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings before the start of trading on Wednesday, Jan. 21. Analysts are predicting that the Texas-based company will report earnings of 55 cents per share for the quarter, a decrease from 70 cents per share in the same period last year. The consensus among analysts is that Halliburton's quarterly revenue will total $5.41 billion, down from $5.61 billion in the previous year. Despite this, the company has surpassed revenue expectations in the last three quarters, but has only outperformed in four of the past 10 quarters overall. Following this news, shares of Halliburton saw a 1.6% decline, closing at $32.06 on Tuesday. For further insights, interested individuals can find more information on analyst ratings on the Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: Stocks to watch: Netflix, Johnson & Johnson. JNJ expected to report $2.46 EPS on $24.16B revenue. GameStop shares rise.
data.description: U.S. stock futures are showing a rise this morning, with investor attention likely to focus on certain stocks. Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) is expected to announce quarterly earnings of $2.46 per share with revenue reaching $24.16 billion before the market opens. The company's shares saw a slight decline of 0.3% to $217.50 in after-hours trading. In extended trading on Tuesday, GameStop Corp. (NYSE: GME) experienced an increase in shares following CEO Ryan Cohen's disclosure. More details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson to report Q4 earnings of $2.47 per share on Jan. 21, with revenue estimate at $24.16 billion.
data.description: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings on Wednesday, Jan. 21. Analysts anticipate earnings of $2.47 per share, up from $2.04 per share in the same period last year. The consensus estimate for the company's quarterly revenue is $24.16 billion compared to $22.52 billion last year. In recent news, on Jan. 14, Johnson & Johnson unveiled preliminary findings from the Phase 3 MajesTEC-9 study of Tecvayli for the treatment of multiple myeloma. The company's shares closed at $218.21 on Tuesday, experiencing a 0.2% drop. Readers can find more details on analyst ratings at Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stock futures rose after Trump's Greenland threat, breaking key support for S&P 500 and Nasdaq. Nvidia and Tesla dropped.
data.description: Major stock indices including the S&P 500 and Nasdaq experienced a significant drop following President Trump's statements regarding Greenland, with expectations building on his speech at Davos. Tech giants like Nvidia and Tesla saw a decline alongside other megacap companies. Netflix also showed a late drop in its stock value. The Dow Jones Futures show a rise after the sell-off triggered by the President's Greenland threats, with Tesla and Nvidia being among the companies that experienced a decline in their stocks.
